tactical_shape: Underdogs who defended deep at times and tried to stay in the game
Relied on quick moments and set-piece threat rather than sustained control
standout_players: Ali al-Hamadi — had a decent early chance, scooped over the bar
Ali Jasim — found Amir al-Ammari for Iraq’s equaliser build-up
Amir al-Ammari — stood up the cross for Hussein’s header
Aymen Hussein — scored a magnificent headed equaliser, Iraq’s finest World Cup moment
Ibrahim Bayesh — unlucky to be denied by Møller Wolfe’s block
Akam Hashem — half-volley just over the bar
Jalal Hassan — at fault for Norway’s second after pausing on a back-pass
home_read.momentum: Took huge credit from pushing Norway hard and producing a historic World Cup equaliser, but the closing outlook is still gloomy with expectations they may finish bottom of the group.

tactical_shape: Front-foot attacking side with Haaland as the focal point
Used wide service and high passing, with Nusa and Møller Wolfe supplying chances
standout_players: Erling Haaland — match-defining double, ruthless finisher, 11 goals in his last 11 recent competitive Norway games
Antonio Nusa — particularly lively, bamboozled Iraq with his touch and supplied the opening danger
Martin Ødegaard — less visible than Haaland but created Norway’s third goal
David Møller Wolfe — provided the cross for Haaland’s opener
Leo Østigård — scored from Ødegaard’s corner
Kristoffer Ajer — delivered the cross for Haaland’s stoppage-time own-goal forcing header
Oscar Bobb — named among the substitutes and described as a player who would walk into decent international teams
Ørjan Håskjold Nyland — beat a header from Hussein but not much else is said positively
away_read.momentum: A win built around Haaland gives Norway a strong platform, but the punditry warns they still need to improve, especially defensively, before sterner tests.
